candidate_summary: CYNGHANEDD-MIRROR conjunction: (a) K-class discrete codebook over token embeddings (VQ) + (b) paired-sentence pretraining corpus + (c) ordered position-by-position class-sequence match as auxiliary pretraining loss.

results_analysis.title: Vector-Quantized Prompt Learning for Paraphrase Generation (EMNLP 2023)
results_analysis.reason: Combines VQ discrete codebook over prompt-token embeddings with paraphrase (paired-sentence) generation, capturing components (a)+(b) of the conjunction; lacks only the ordered class-sequence symmetry loss (c), so functional similarity exceeds 0.7.

results_analysis.title: Vector-Quantized Prompt Learning Paraphrase (arxiv 2023)
results_analysis.reason: Same artefact as rank 1 (VQ codebook + paraphrase pretraining); two of three conjuncts directly present, so semantic/functional thresholds are crossed.

results_analysis.title: Token Order Prediction (TOP) auxiliary objective
results_analysis.reason: Ordered auxiliary token-ranking loss shares the 'ordered auxiliary loss' theme but lacks a discrete codebook and paired-sentence symmetry, so it sits below both thresholds.

results_analysis.title: Beyond Multi-Token Prediction: Pretraining with Future Summaries (arxiv 2510)
results_analysis.reason: Future-summary auxiliary loss is not symmetric paired-sentence and uses no discrete codebook, missing two of three conjuncts.

results_analysis.title: Multi-Token Prediction Needs Registers (OpenReview)
results_analysis.reason: Generic multi-token prediction with register tokens; no codebook, no paired-sentence symmetry, no class-sequence match.

results_analysis.title: Training LLMs Beyond Next Token Prediction: Filling the Mutual Information Gap (arxiv 2511)
results_analysis.reason: MI-gap auxiliary loss is generic beyond-NTP; lacks codebook and paired symmetric structure.

results_analysis.title: Addressing Representation Collapse in VQ Models with One Linear Layer (ICCV 2025)
results_analysis.reason: VQ utilization fix in isolation; no paired-sentence pretraining or class-sequence symmetry component.

results_analysis.title: Not All Tokens Are What You Need for Pretraining (OpenReview)
results_analysis.reason: Selective token-weighting pretraining; no codebook, no pair symmetry, no ordered class-sequence loss.

verdict_sentence: Two independent hits (ranks 1 and 2, the Vector-Quantized Prompt Learning for Paraphrase Generation paper) cross both the semantic (>=0.7) and functional (>=0.7) thresholds by combining a VQ discrete codebook with paired-sentence paraphrase pretraining, so the candidate fails novelty.
